data.title: MagneRide Dampers Achieves Optimal Safety, Balances Handling and Comfort Through Hardware-Software Innovation
data.description: BWI Group, a prominent player in the smart suspension systems sector, has unveiled its latest innovation - the fourth-generation MagneRide damper. This cutting-edge technology combines wheel-end acceleration sensors with inertial measurement units (IMU), enabling seamless integration of software-based control and advanced sensing capabilities. The MagneRide damper boasts an impressive damping adjustment bandwidth, operating at speeds of up to 1000 times per second. Additionally, its adaptable modular design tackles crucial issues in ensuring vehicle safety and optimizing handling performance simultaneously.

data.title: Teledyne Labtech Accelerates Prototype and Early‑Programme PCB Builds with New Rapid Quote‑to‑Manufacture Service
data.description: Teledyne Labtech, based in PRESTEIGNE, Wales, has recently launched a new service called Rapid Quote‑to‑Manufacture. This service is aimed at accelerating the process from quotation to delivery of prototype PCBs for early-stage RF and microwave projects. In response to customer input highlighting production delays, the service offers quicker pricing, decreased lead times, and a more reliable transition from purchase order to manufacture. Teledyne Labtech is recognized for its expertise in microwave circuit design, component manufacturing, and testing.

data.title: AI Is Elevating UK Tech Roles Rather Than Taking Jobs, as UK Sits 15th out of 34 Countries for Overall Tech Salaries
data.description: A report from Hays’ Tech Talent Explorer delves into the impact of AI on tech roles worldwide, emphasizing its ability to enhance human skills. The study compares salaries for key tech positions on a global scale, revealing the UK's strong standing against other countries. In the UK, tech roles demonstrate a medium level of AI influence, with AI supplementing rather than supplanting human expertise. UK tech experts are paid competitively on a global scale, ranking 15th out of 34 countries in average salaries.

data.title: Bitcoin aims to break $75,000 resistance by end of April. Price at $71,804 with RSI of 58.8, predicts $75,794 if support stays above $68,770.
data.description: The current price of Bitcoin is experiencing a bullish trend, reaching $71,804, with the Relative Strength Index (RSI) standing at 58.8. Based on technical analysis, there is a possibility of a potential surge towards the $75,794 resistance level if the current support remains above $68,770.

data.title: Baseload's latest book brings the energy transition into the classroom
data.description: On April 8, 2026, in Stockholm, Baseload introduced their new children's book series, Our Hidden Powers, emphasizing the importance of tackling the climate crisis from an early age. The second book in the series, titled The Big..., underscores this message. This initiative aims to educate young minds on environmental issues, promoting awareness and action. By starting this conversation early, Baseload hopes to instill a sense of responsibility in the next generation.

data.title: Stablecoin supply on Ethereum reaches $180B peak. Predicted $850B in new flows by 2030 if current trend persists.
data.description: By 2030, Ethereum could potentially attract around $850 billion in new investments, as per Token Terminal's analysis of the current trend.

data.title: 3 traders on Polymarket bet on US-Iran ceasefire; odds ranged from 2.9% to 10.3%. They made bets within 26 hours of news.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, bets indicating a positive outcome were made with probabilities ranging from 2.9% to 10.3%. The three wallets placing these bets did so within a span of 26 hours following the announcement.

data.title: US prosecutors deny dismissal of charges against Tornado Cash co-founder; faces possible retrial on two charges after 2025 hung jury.
data.description: The co-founder of a crypto mixing service, Roman Storm, is currently awaiting a possible retrial on two charges after a jury was unable to reach a verdict in 2025. The charges against him stem from undisclosed activities related to the service. It remains uncertain when the retrial will take place, and Storm continues to face legal uncertainty surrounding the case.
